content.text:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.

Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.
- /workspace/.seeds/issues.jsonl holds the project's issue queue.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Committing is mandatory, not the same as staging. `git add` ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH — you must run `git commit` so the work lands as a real commit. A run that ends with staged-but-uncommitted changes is treated as a FAILURE (`dropped_commit`), not a success. Before you report completion, run `git status`/`git log` and confirm your changes are in a commit, not just staged. The only exception is when you have genuinely made no file changes at all.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

---

work on sd warren-5f07. use ml. commit when done.

# Project Expertise Manifest (via Mulch)

> Manifest mode lists available domains. Load records on demand with `ml prime <domain>` or `ml prime --files <path>`.

## Quick Reference

- `ml prime <domain>` — load full records for one domain
- `ml prime --files <path>` — load records relevant to specific files
- `ml search "<query>"` — search records across domains
- `ml record <domain> --type <type> --description "..."` — store an insight
- `ml learn` — discover what to record from changed files
- `ml sync` — validate, stage, and commit .mulch/ changes

## Available Domains

- **build**: 106 records (5 patterns, 72 conventions, 17 failures, 7 decisions, 5 references) — updated just now — approaching limit

---SD PRIME---
# Seeds Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `sd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session

# Session Close Protocol

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. Close completed issues:    sd close <id1> <id2> ...
[ ] 2. File issues for remaining:  sd create --title "..."
[ ] 3. Run quality gates:          bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck
[ ] 4. Sync and push:              sd sync && git push
[ ] 5. Verify:                     git status (must show "up to date with origin")
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use seeds for ALL task tracking (`sd create`, `sd ready`, `sd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create issues BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- Git workflow: run `sd sync` at session end

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `sd ready` — Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `sd list --status=open` — All open issues
- `sd list --status=in_progress` — Your active work
- `sd show <id> [<id2> ...]` — Detailed issue view; multi-id shows each separated by a divider (`--json` returns `issues: [...]`)

### Creating & Updating
- `sd create --title="..." --type=task|bug|feature|epic --priority=2` — New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog)
- `sd update <id> --status=in_progress` — Claim work
- `sd update <id> --assignee=username` — Assign to someone
- `sd close <id>` — Mark complete
- `sd close <id1> <id2> ...` — Close multiple issues at once

content.text:
## db (2 records, updated just now)

### Conventions
- [mx-da4381] plan_runs.parent_run_id (sqlite migration 0018_add_plan_run_parent_run_id, postgres migration 0011_add_plan_run_parent_run_id, warren-d9a2) is a nullable TEXT column back-linking an auto_plan_run to the run that created it. No FK — the coordinator handles missing parent rows (treat as gate-passed). CreatePlanRunInput gained optional parentRunId field; reap passes run.id when creating auto_plan_runs.
- [mx-42d460] Postgres count(*) is bigint, returned as string by node-postgres — repo code MUST coerce with Number() before returning a number to callers. Drizzle does not auto-cast bigint columns. EventsRepo.countByRun (and earlier RunsRepo.countInflightByWorker) take this shape: pickOne<{ n: number | string }>(...) then return Number(row?.n ?? 0). Same applies to any sum()/count() aggregate; max() over an integer column already returns number, no coercion needed. [relates to: mx-1d9f7a]

## registry (20 records, updated just now)

### Conventions
- [mx-65509f] pr-fixer builtin (warren-05ea) is the first source-editing builtin without auto_plan_run frontmatter — the CI poller dispatches it directly rather than reap's plan-run path. runtime='pi', network='open'. System prompt forbids opening a new PR (warren pushes to the existing PR branch) and forbids skip/delete of failing tests as a workaround.
- [mx-20a3b9] AgentDefinition.frontmatter.provider and .model are the well-known optional string fields for the multi-provider surface (warren-f8c0). They live in the open frontmatter bag — no schema rev. readProviderFrontmatter() narrows them to typed strings; withProviderOverrides() folds operator-supplied per-run overrides onto a freshly-cloned AgentDefinition before spawnRun freezes runs.rendered_agent_json. Empty/whitespace overrides are no-ops; the base agent row is untouched (override is per-run, not per-agent).
- [mx-914240] seedBuiltinAgents re-upserts pre-existing built-in rows if their content/frontmatter has drifted from code, while preserving overrides from library and project tiers
- [mx-9996bb] readRuntimeId(agent) (src/registry/schema.ts, warren-ebca) is the seam src/runs/spawn.ts uses to resolve the burrow runtime id for dispatch: prefers frontmatter.runtime (string, non-empty) and falls back to agent.name. System-prompt-only canopy agents whose name does NOT match a BUILT_IN_RUNTIMES entry (brainstorm/planner — and any future library agent that layers on top of claude-code/sapling/pi) MUST set frontmatter.runtime, or burrow rejects dispatch with 'agent <name> is not registered' and warren reaps as never_started. claude-code/sapling/pi builtins keep name=runtime convention and need no stamp.
- [mx-f52e13] Built-in agents encode provenance via renderedJson.frontmatter.source = 'builtin'; canopy-loaded agents leave it unset (canopy doesn't emit it). readAgentSource() in src/registry/builtins/index.ts is the single reader; the HTTP /agents endpoint uses it to attach a 'source' field per row.
- [mx-4888d2] PI_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/pi.ts) ships the same parity shape as SAPLING_BUILTIN (system + burrow_config sections, network='open'). The pi-specific surfaces — multi-provider override (frontmatter.provider/model), cost tracking (runs.cost_usd), .pi/skills + .pi/prompts materialization, pi-namespaced event kinds — are layered in follow-on steps of pl-4374 (warren-846b/f8c0/a7dc/70af/f1da), not in this builtin file.
- [mx-a57bac] cn --json show <name> returns the un-resolved prompt envelope {success, command:'show', prompt:{...}} including extends/mixins/sections/frontmatter; not-found exits non-zero with a structured {success:false, error: "Prompt 'X' not found"} (single quotes) or 'Prompt "X" not found' (cn render — double quotes). CanopyClient.showAgent collapses the not-found envelope to null so the compose resolver can fall through tiers without string-matching elsewhere.
- [mx-db68df] AgentSource is a three-tier union (R-03, pl-fef5): 'builtin' (inline built-ins), 'library' (canopy library and the unset-frontmatter fallback), and 'project:<projectId>' (project-tier .canopy/ rows, stamped at render time). Read provenance with readAgentSource(renderedJson); classify coarse tier with agentSourceTier(source); extract the project id with projectIdFromAgentSource(source); construct a project source with makeProjectAgentSource(projectId) (rejects empty). Refresh helpers overlay frontmatter.source via stampAgentSource(agent, source) — original AgentDefinition is not mutated. readAgentSource collapses 'project:' with empty suffix back to 'library' so a malformed row doesn't masquerade as the project tier. [relates to: mx-f52e13]
- [mx-f999d0] PLANNER_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/planner.ts, warren-543d / pl-0344 step 7) is the second interactive built-in agent, pairing with brainstorm. Tagged 'agent' + 'interactive' in frontmatter (same as brainstorm) so the UI interactive-run picker can filter without parsing the system prompt. burrow_config sets [sandbox].network = 'open' for web scouting; richer per-tool ACLs aren't wired through src/runs/burrow_config.ts yet, so the path-scoped write contract ('.plot/' and '.seeds/' only, no source files, no dispatch, no git writes) is enforced in the system prompt. Registered in BUILTIN_AGENTS in src/registry/builtins/index.ts. [relates to: mx-77117c]
- [mx-39a64f] Adding a new built-in agent (alongside claude-code/sapling/pi) requires updates in five places in lockstep: (1) src/registry/builtins/<name>.ts with the AgentDefinition (mirror SAPLING_BUILTIN shape: name, version=1, sections.system + sections.burrow_config, resolvedFrom=['builtin:<name>'], frontmatter.source='builtin'); (2) src/registry/builtins/index.ts BUILTIN_AGENTS array + re-export; (3) src/registry/builtins/builtins.test.ts assertions on BUILTIN_AGENT_NAMES + readAgentSource; (4) scripts/acceptance/scenarios/13-container-smoke.ts builtin-names check; (5) src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx header + empty-state copy. Note: scenario 02-agents-refresh.ts checks 'no library-sourced agents before refresh' via filter source !== 'builtin', so it auto-extends without a code change.
- [mx-7c0df0] BRAINSTORM_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/brainstorm.ts, pl-0344 step 6 / warren-3de8) is the first interactive built-in agent — registered in BUILTIN_AGENTS alongside claude-code/sapling/pi. It's a read-only scout: warren's burrow_config only forwards [sandbox].network (src/runs/burrow_config.ts), so the no-writes/no-dispatch contract is enforced in the system prompt, not the sandbox profile. Sets network = "open" for web-fetch scouting and tags frontmatter with ["agent", "interactive"] so the UI can filter interactive-mode pickers. Prompt drives the user toward the four Plot intent fields (goal / non_goals / constraints / success_criteria) — fixture tests in builtins.test.ts pin those strings so a casual edit can't silently widen the role.
- [mx-91d2b5] LEVERET_BUILTIN (src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts, warren-fdd9) is the first 'conversation'-tagged built-in and the first with runtime='pi-chat' — readRuntimeId forwards frontmatter.runtime as a free string so no KNOWN_RUNTIME_IDS change is needed. It ships its single pi extension via the sections.pi_extensions JSONL {name,body} line (seeded to .pi/extensions/propose_intent.ts by buildSeedFiles). The propose_intent tool is a pure proposal carrier: execute() returns the field-scoped intent patch ({goal,non_goals,constraints,success_criteria}) on result.details and writes nothing — warren correlates it host-side via tool_execution_end toolCallId into intent_edited(actor=leveret). The def grants read-leaning tools only (read/grep/find/ls/bash, NO edit/write) so the send-off PR is plot-state-only by construction.
- [mx-e74f25] refreshProjectAgents (src/registry/refresh.ts, pl-fef5 step 5 / warren-91bd) is the project-tier counterpart to refreshAgentRegistry: takes a CanopyClient.forProjectPath(...), lists+renders prompts from <projectPath>/.canopy/, calls stampAgentSource with makeProjectAgentSource(projectId) to overlay frontmatter.source='project:<id>', and upserts at projectId scope so global rows are untouched. Per-agent failures collected into RefreshSkipped (same shape as library refresh); transport-level cn errors throw (the all-projects loop in step 6 catches them). Prune is always-on (not opt-in like the library), because .canopy/ is the authoritative source for the project tier — listForProject(projectId) is diffed against the seen-set and missing rows deleted via repo.delete(name, {projectId}). Return shape is {projectId, registered, skipped, removed} matching the planned POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh response. [relates to: mx-76344d]
- [mx-df1290] On-disk rendered cache for project-tier agents (warren-44e3, follow-up to R-03/pl-fef5): refreshProjectAgents (src/registry/refresh.ts) accepts optional projectPath + cacheWriter. When projectPath is set, init() once seeds <projectPath>/.canopy/.rendered/.gitignore (single line '*\n' — self-ignoring so the cache stays out of project commits), write() per registered agent dumps the stamped AgentDefinition (frontmatter.source = 'project:<id>' included) as pretty-printed JSON to .canopy/.rendered/<name>.json, prune() per removed row rm-fs the matching file. defaultRenderedCacheWriter validates names against /^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$/ — unsafe names skip the fs write (the agents-table row is still the authoritative cache). handlers.ts (refreshAgents loop and refreshProjectAgentsHandler) now pass projectPath: project.localPath. Tests that create projects with a localPath must use a real writable tmpdir, not a sentinel path like /data/projects/x/y. [relates to: mx-e74f25]

### Patterns
- [mx-7a2c34] **cross-tier compose fallback for project-tier roles**: When cn render fails for a project-tier role because extends/mixins reference a name outside the project's .canopy/, refreshProjectAgents (src/registry/refresh.ts) falls back to a warren-side composer (src/registry/compose.ts) that fetches the raw prompt via cn show --json, walks parents through a resolver that tries project tier first (cn show) then global tier (agents.get), and merges sections + frontmatter using canopy's own algorithm (parent first, mixins left-to-right, focal last; empty-body removes; later overrides earlier). Source stamp tracks the LEAF tier (project:<id>); resolvedFrom preserves the full chain. (.seeds/issues.jsonl, CHANGELOG.md, ROADMAP.md, src/registry/canopy.test.ts, src/registry/canopy.ts, src/registry/refresh.test.ts, src/registry/refresh.ts, src/registry/schema.ts) [relates to: mx-df1290]

### Known Failures
- [mx-d2dd02] seedBuiltinAgents (src/registry/builtins/index.ts:82-86) early-returns when a row with the builtin's name already exists. On upgrades that change a builtin's renderedJson (e.g. adding a frontmatter field like warren-ebca's frontmatter.runtime), the stale v_prev row sticks around — the new code reads cached agents from the DB via readCachedAgent, so the source-side fix is dead text on every existing install. Symptom on warren-deployed: brainstorm/planner dispatch still hit 'agent X is not registered' after v0.5.1 boot even though src/registry/builtins/{brainstorm,planner}.ts had been patched and shipped.
  → Hand-patch via UPDATE agents SET rendered_json = jsonb_set(rendered_json, '{frontmatter,runtime}', '"claude-code"'::jsonb, true) WHERE name IN ('brainstorm','planner'). Permanent fix tracked as warren-ced0: re-upsert when existing.frontmatter.source === 'builtin', preserving library/project tier overrides.

Fix: coerceBooleanFlag() accepts real boolean true OR case-insensitive trimmed string \"true\"; \"false\"/other => false. Apply the same defensive coercion to any boolean-ish frontmatter flag read from rendered_agent_json." 2>&1 | tail -3
